Secret Incident 1234.0

Location: Office Kitchen – Company Refrigerator (aka James)
Date: [Redacted]
Reported by: ██████

Incident Description:
An employee placed a yogurt in the office refrigerator. Upon returning, the yogurt was missing. A warning sign labeled “Do Not Touch” was placed, but the yogurt disappeared again. Consultations with coworkers confirmed that no one had eaten it.

Over the following days, all newly stored foods began to vanish from the refrigerator, including sandwiches, snacks, and other perishable items. However, certain items—specifically cheese and bananas—remained untouched, regardless of placement.

Employee Interviews:

██████ (Accounting): “I didn’t touch anyone’s food. I thought maybe someone was playing a prank, but even when I watched the fridge, the food kept disappearing.”

██████ (Marketing): “I swear, I only eat my own lunch. I even put my sandwich on a separate shelf. The next time I checked, it was gone.”

██████ (IT): “At first, we thought it was a thief among us. Then we started noticing that the sign ‘Do Not Touch’ stayed in place, but the food vanished anyway. That’s when we realized… it had to be James.”

Discovery of James’ Activity:
Employees conducted a series of observations. They left different types of food in the fridge and monitored the door using a hidden camera. The footage revealed that whenever the door was closed and the office was empty, the food would slowly disappear. Cheese and bananas remained intact, suggesting selective consumption by James.

Further tests included leaving small notes, color-coded markers, and even non-food items. Only edible items besides cheese and bananas disappeared. It became clear that the refrigerator itself was actively consuming the stored food whenever the door was closed.

Resolution:
A new warning sign, “Do Not Touch – This is James”, was posted prominently. After implementation, no further food disappeared. Employees resumed using the refrigerator normally, with cheese and bananas remaining the only items immune to consumption.

Notes:

Cheese and bananas appear immune to James’ activity.

No further incidents reported after the warning sign was implemented.

Incident confirmed as anomalous but contained.

Status: Closed
